pr.title: Cleanup statics in vi, match and link vi1202
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Split the crowd sound-effect code out of vi1202 into a new melee/sfx/crowdsfx translation unit, updated split/symbol/config metadata, moved public crowd SFX declarations into a dedicated header, and cleaned vi static JObj helper duplication by using existing HSD_JObj*WithMtxDirty helpers. The PR made vi1202 a Matching object and linked 25 crowd SFX items under the new sfx/crowdsfx unit, with decomp.dev reporting +2036 linked code bytes.
what_changed: Shortened the vi1202 split range from 0x8032110C-0x80322620 to 0x8032110C-0x80321900 and added a new melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c split covering text 0x80321900-0x80322620 plus the related bss, sbss, and sdata2 ranges.

Added src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c with the extracted crowd reaction / crowd SFX functions from 0x80321900 onward, including un_80321900, fn_803219AC, un_80321A00, un_80321AF4, un_80321D30, un_80321EBC, un_8032201C, un_8032233C, un_803224DC, and un_80322598.
Added src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.h containing CrowdSFX_UnkStruct, CrowdConfig, gCrowdConfig, and prototypes for the extracted crowd SFX functions.
Removed CrowdConfig and crowd SFX function declarations from vi1202.h, leaving vi1202.h focused on vi1202 scene functions.
Changed fighter and game-mode includes that used vi/vi1202.h for crowd functions to include sfx/crowdsfx.h instead.
Changed configure.py so melee/vi/vi1202.c is built as Matching and added a new MeleeLib entry for sfx (Sound effects) containing melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c as NonMatching.
Updated symbols.txt for vi1202-local data/sdata/sdata2 labels, converting several previously global un_/lbl_ symbols to local @ labels around 0x804003A0, 0x804D5B30, and 0x804DE138.

Removed duplicated static HSD_JObjSet* wrapper functions from vi1201v1, vi1201v2, and vi1202, replacing calls with existing HSD_JObjSetTranslate*WithMtxDirty, HSD_JObjSetRotationYWithMtxDirty, and HSD_JObjSetScale*WithMtxDirty helpers.
In vi1202.c, introduced a small static vi1202_SetupChild helper to group repeated child JObj translation, rotation, and scale setup while preserving matching with a PAD_STACK adjustment.
smart_moves: Separated the crowd SFX logic into sfx/crowdsfx because the PR author stated that matching vi1202 required a split and the new file made logical sense; this reduced vi1202 to the actual vi scene portion and let vi1202 become Matching.

Kept address-based unknown function names for extracted routines while adding semantic type names and comments for CrowdConfig, avoiding unsupported full renaming while still improving future searchability.
Moved downstream includes from vi1202.h to crowdsfx.h, which prevents fighter and game-mode code from depending on a vi scene header just to call crowd SFX routines.
Used existing HSD_JObj*WithMtxDirty API helpers instead of duplicated local static wrappers, reducing local statics and avoiding repeated assert/dirty-matrix boilerplate.

Preserved matching-sensitive code patterns in crowdsfx.c, including #pragma dont_inline, M2C_FIELD accesses, PAD_STACK, gotos, and small inline/static caller helpers where the diff shows they were needed.
Updated splits.txt, symbols.txt, and configure.py together with the source move, avoiding a source/config mismatch for the new translation unit.
decomp_lessons: When a scene file contains a logically separate system at a contiguous address range, a translation-unit split can be the cleanest route to both better ownership and matching progress.
Header ownership matters: declarations for a gameplay/audio subsystem should live with that subsystem, not in a scene header used only incidentally by callers.

Before duplicating SDK-style static helpers to force codegen, check whether shared helpers already exist with the same dirty-matrix behavior; replacing duplicates can clean up statics without losing match.
A split requires coordinated edits to splits.txt, symbols.txt, configure.py, source files, and include users; partial changes can make progress accounting misleading or break dependencies.
Local data symbols in symbols.txt should be scoped as local @ labels when evidence indicates they belong to one object rather than globally named un_/lbl_ symbols.
Semantic struct naming can be justified with external map evidence and behavior comments, even if function names remain address-based pending stronger symbol evidence.
naming_conventions: New subsystem path uses src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.c and src/melee/sfx/crowdsfx.h for crowd sound effects.
Header guard for the moved vi header was normalized from GALE01_32110C to MELEE_VI_VI1202_H.
The new crowd header uses MELEE_CROWD_CROWDSFX_H as its guard, though the path is melee/sfx/crowdsfx.h.
Types introduced or moved use Crowd-prefixed names: CrowdSFX_UnkStruct and CrowdConfig.
Unknown functions retained address-style names such as un_80321900 and fn_803219AC, while parameter names like spawn_id, pos_x, kb_mag, mag, and angle were used where behavior was clearer.

CrowdConfig fields mix semantic names where inferred, such as kb_threshold_low, cheer_limit, max_gasp_count, horiz_margin, fighters_near_blastzone, and blastzone_y_offset, with xNN placeholders where names remain uncertain.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Created a new object boundary at 0x80321900 so vi1202 text ends before the crowd SFX manager code and crowdsfx.c owns 0x80321900-0x80322620.

Left crowdsfx.c as NonMatching in configure.py while vi1202.c became Matching, allowing the split to link extracted functions without claiming the new unit is fully source-matched in the build config.
Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around un_80321C70 and un_80321CA4, indicating inlining control was matching-sensitive.
Kept an inline-like static copy un_80321C70_inline and static caller un_80321CE8_caller to reproduce call/inlining behavior around repeated audio stop/play patterns.
Preserved M2C_FIELD accesses to mpLib_80458868 offsets 0x14, 0x18, and 0x1C where stronger typing was not yet established.
Used PAD_STACK adjustments in vi1202_OnEnter and PAD_STACK in crowdsfx functions to maintain stack layout.
Converted many vi1202 data symbols to local @ labels in symbols.txt after the split, matching object-local data ownership.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.

The decomp.dev bot reported linked code improved to 42.41% (+0.05%, +2036 bytes), linked data improved to 35.01% (+0.02%, +192 bytes), and matched data slightly decreased to 41.43% (-0.01%, -144 bytes).

The bot listed 25 new matches under main/melee/sfx/crowdsfx, including .bss and .sbss, while showing the same routines as broken matches under main/melee/vi/vi1202 because they moved object ownership.
The bot also noted unmatched-item regressions for vi1201v1 .data and vi1201v2 .data/on-enter metrics; the slice does not include human discussion of these small regressions.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename un_80321900+ CrowdSFXManager functions or improve CrowdSFX_UnkStruct field names.
Check whether crowdsfx.c was later changed from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py.
Search for subsequent cleanup of mpLib_80458868 M2C_FIELD accesses in crowd SFX out-of-bounds and blastzone checks.
Look for PRs resolving the small vi1201v1/vi1201v2 data regressions noted by decomp.dev after this split.
Search for consistency changes to the MELEE_CROWD_CROWDSFX_H guard versus the sfx path naming.
